closures.gap_source: docs/meta-audit/cycle-08/B-attempt-01.md §'Scratch notes' (no preliminaries lemma; the claim 'rule LHSs have heads in {add, mul, q, t, neg}' is stated inline in Q1's NF check rather than extracted as a reusable lemma cited by Q2's uniqueness argument)
closures.gap_description: attempt-01 lacks the preliminary catalogue of rule-LHS heads + immediate-NF catalogue used by both Q1 and Q2; the same head-enumeration is implicitly performed twice.
closures.closure_location: B-ARGUMENT.md §1.1 'Rule LHS heads' + §1.2 'Immediate NF catalogue' + §1.3 'Subterm/closure conventions' — three preliminary sub-sections cited by §2.2 (Q1 NF check) and §3.2/§3.3 (Q2 uniqueness theorem)
closures.non_inflation_note: Lemma extraction strengthens the deliverable; the cited inline claims become citations to a single proof. No new gap.

closures.gap_source: docs/meta-audit/cycle-08/B-attempt-01.md 'Q2 — non-WN' subsection (claims uniqueness of redex prose-style: 'the only redex in t(s^n(0)) is alpha7 at root because no LHS matches s or 0'; treated as obvious; no formal lemma statement)
closures.gap_description: attempt-01 hand-waves the load-bearing uniqueness-of-redex claim. The deliverable obligation requires a rigorous proof since the entire Q2 verdict rests on it.
closures.closure_location: B-ARGUMENT.md §3.2 'The crux: uniqueness of redex at t(s^n(0))' (formal lemma statement + per-LHS rejection enumeration over the 8 rules) + §3.3 'The unique-reduction theorem' (induction over reduction-sequence length) — load-bearing claim now has a formal lemma + theorem with full proof
closures.non_inflation_note: Formalising the lemma did not introduce new high-severity gaps. (One CONDITIONAL: the lemma uses 'no rule LHS has head s or 0' which is verified inline by case analysis of the 8 rules; this dependency is fully discharged.)

closures.gap_source: docs/meta-audit/cycle-08/B-attempt-01.md 'Q1 — non-confluence' subsection (states 'instantiating x:=0, y:=e gives the concrete closed term q(0,e)' but does not generalise to a structural statement about q-projection)
closures.gap_description: attempt-01 stops at the single-instance witness for Q1; no claim about whether q's non-confluence pattern generalises (a structural / parametric observation that strengthens the deliverable's Q1 reasoning).
closures.closure_location: B-ARGUMENT.md §2.5 'Structural reason (non-essential, for completeness)' — adds the parametric observation that 'any pair of rules with shape g(v_1,...,v_n) -> v_i and g(v_1,...,v_n) -> v_j with i != j produces a non-joining critical pair (v_i, v_j) at the root', generalising the q-specific witness
closures.non_inflation_note: Structural generalisation is additive; no new gaps.

closures.gap_source: docs/meta-audit/cycle-08/B-attempt-01.md 'Q3 — non-SN' subsection (single sentence noting 'Q3 follows from Q2 since the unique reduction is already infinite', no explicit step-by-step infinite sequence with rule citation per step)
closures.gap_description: attempt-01 reduces Q3 to Q2 informally; the TASK constraint requires a SPECIFIC infinite reduction sequence with the rule applied at each step explicitly named.
closures.closure_location: B-ARGUMENT.md §4.1 'Witness' + §4.2 'Verification that each step is a legal rewrite' + §4.3 'Pattern made explicit' + §4.4 'The general termination impossibility' + §4.5 'Conclusion for Q3' — Q3 now has its own explicit step-by-step trace t(0) -> t(s(0)) -> t(s(s(0))) -> ... with per-step alpha7 citation and inductive argument that the pattern is unbounded
closures.non_inflation_note: Explicit Q3 trace closes the constraint requirement. No new gap.
